annotation_basis: Blind annotation from source O&M text only.

concept_ground_truth.label: T1
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Begin the leak map on Aurex E-Motion-412 LR by recording battery coolant level, residue color, undertray wetness, recent thermal-system repair history, and whether seepage is already visible at the left coolant-plate outlet while the pack remains in the as-received condition with no covers moved.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T1 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T2
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Power the vehicle down, isolate the high-voltage pack, and allow the local cooling branch to equalize. Then remove the rear underbody shield and the left pack-side splash cover while keeping the hose angle unchanged so the original quick-coupler seating witness can still be read.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T2 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T3
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Wipe only the loose surface film first, leave the wet edge untouched, and trace the leak path from the coolant-plate seam toward the outlet hose, the quick-coupler lock window, the O-ring seat, and the nearby bracket drain edge so the actual origin is not blurred by cleaning too early.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T3 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T4
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Install the local pressure tool on the coolant-plate inlet and outlet, cap the neighboring branch, and hold the specified pressure long enough to observe whether fresh wetting begins at the plate seam, the coupler body, the hose clamp line, or the tube insertion stop inside the connector.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T4 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T5
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Compare the suspect joint with the opposite coolant-plate branch, paying attention to hose twist, clamp clocking, connector latch height, tube insertion depth, and any side-load mark where the support bracket may have forced the plastic coupler body off-axis. This comparison should distinguish a deformed connector body from a hose-routing load or a clamp-position error before the joint is opened.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T5 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T6
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Inspect the aluminum plate edge, the molded quick connector, the green O-ring land, the hose end, and the nearby harness clip for dried coolant crystals, abrasion, plastic stress whitening, or metal staining that would separate connector leakage from a plate-side crack.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T6 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T7
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Mark the confirmed leak boundary directly at the leaking hardware, photograph the coolant-plate seam and the quick-coupler seat from more than one angle, and note clearly whether the failure belongs to the plate seam, the O-ring interface, the connector shell, or the hose-routing condition.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T7 retained verbatim from the source document.

concept_ground_truth.label: T8
concept_ground_truth.surface_form: Refit only the access parts needed to protect the opened area, leave the identified leak point readable for downstream repair, and close the record with the exact hardware named as battery coolant-plate outlet quick connector or coolant-plate seam instead of leaving a generic leak description. The accepted leak map should still let the next repair start from one named outlet-side boundary rather than from a broad pack-coolant leak note.
concept_ground_truth.reason: O&M step T8 retained verbatim from the source document.
